mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 5.0%, labor market slack dissipates, triggering wage-price pressures and boosting corporate revenue expectations, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The ratio of real GDP growth to industrial production growth captures demand-supply imbalances; when GDP outpaces INDPRO, it signals excess demand that lifts corporate margins and equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no significant correlation with the GDPC1/INDPRO growth differential, or the signal flips direction during recessions without regime adjustment.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, rising real rates compress equity multiples; however, when CPIAUCSL remains sticky above 3.0% while FEDFUNDS rises, the market prices in a soft landing, supporting positive 20-day forward returns.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns turn negative when FEDFUNDS rises alongside CPIAUCSL > 3.0%, indicating the market prices in stagflation rather than a soft landing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and consumer spending directly translate to higher corporate revenues; a positive month-over-month change in RSAFS reliably predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Monthly RSAFS increases fail to predict positive 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of observations over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained expansion in industrial production indicates robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost volatility and supports positive S&P 500 equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are flat or negative when INDPRO expands by > 1.0% over three months, indicating supply growth is met by demand destruction.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of nonfarm payrolls growth and housing starts captures the labor-demand feedback loop; when PAYEMS rises while HOUST remains below 600, it signals a labor-constrained economy where wage growth supports corporate pricing power, boosting 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS/HOUST signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k and HOUST stays below 600, indicating wage pressures are pricing in recession risk.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion, diverging GDP growth and rising unemployment signal a decelerating economy; this divergence compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week as recession fears dominate.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of instances where GDPC1 growth slows while UNRATE rises, indicating the market ignores deceleration signals.
